name: T3 Code

runner.notes: Prepare a run worktree, open it in T3 Code, provide prompt.md exactly, then run harness verification.

inclusion: Direct ADE competitor for operating coding agents from a desktop or web control plane.
pricing_model: Open-source control plane with bring-your-own subscriptions.
public_stack_signals: Open-source control plane for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Cursor (README 2026-07-15).
Install via npx t3@latest, desktop releases, winget, Homebrew cask, AUR.
TypeScript monorepo; early project warns of bugs and limited contributions.
setup_notes: Confirm current desktop/web install path before benchmark; docs mention provider CLI auth prerequisites.

sources.notes: Positioning, supported agents, BYO subscription claims.
Source repository, README install paths, and provider auth instructions. Refresh 2026-07-15: MIT TypeScript monorepo, ~14.0k stars, active same-day pushes.
Monorepo scripts, Node engine, pnpm, desktop/web/marketing build surfaces.
Installation docs site still reachable (Mintlify). README also points to in-repo docs/ markdown as primary documentation while public docs mature.

research.positioning: Open-source control plane for coding agents with bring-your-own subscriptions.

packaging: desktop artifacts for macOS, Linux AppImage, Windows NSIS
data: session management data model not mapped in this pass
unknowns: Exact runtime storage and desktop shell framework need source inspection beyond package manifest.

infrastructure: Local/web/desktop control plane over existing agent CLIs.
Provider credentials remain with the user's existing CLI logins (codex login, claude auth login, cursor-agent login, opencode auth login).

orchestration: Single surface for multiple coding agents
Switch models mid-thread according to landing page
workspace_isolation: Worktree/isolation details need confirmation in hands-on run
review_shipping: Diff/commit/PR workflow should be probed; public landing emphasizes agent control more than review guarantees
remote_mobile: No official mobile/remote runtime mapped in this pass
context_memory: Session management claimed by docs index; details need verification
integrations: Provider CLI auth for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor

parity_risks: May have less explicit worktree/review automation than Orca/Kandev; compare raw task completion separately from operational workflow.
Install docs and landing page are moving fast; verify current release path before run.

hypotheses: Open-source community distribution around T3/Theo brand.
Minimal control-plane UX can appeal to developers who do not want a heavy orchestration system.
evidence: Official landing positions it as open-source and BYO-subscription.
GitHub repo publishes provider install/auth instructions.
unknowns: Depth of review, evidence, and reproducibility workflow requires manual benchmark.
